import io
import string
import list

fn parse_ints(_ s: String) -> List<Int>
  mut result: List<Int> = []
  let n = string.len(s)
  mut i = 0
  while i < n
    let b = match string.byte_at(s, i)
    when Some(v)
      v
    when None
      0
    end
    if b == 32 or b == 9 or b == 13 or b == 10
      i = i + 1
    else
      mut sign = 1
      if b == 45
        sign = -1
        i = i + 1
      else if b == 43
        i = i + 1
      end
      mut num = 0
      mut done = false
      while not done
        if i >= n
          done = true
        else
          let c = match string.byte_at(s, i)
          when Some(v)
            v
          when None
            0
          end
          if c >= 48 and c <= 57
            num = num * 10 + (c - 48)
            i = i + 1
          else
            done = true
          end
        end
      end
      result = list.push(result, num * sign)
    end
  end
  result
end

fn binary_search(_ xs: List<Int>, _ target: Int) -> Option<Int>
  mut lo = 0
  mut hi = xs.len() - 1
  mut found: Option<Int> = None
  mut keep = true
  while keep and lo <= hi
    let mid = (lo + hi) / 2
    let v = match xs.get(mid)
    when Some(x)
      x
    when None
      0
    end
    if v == target
      found = Some(mid)
      keep = false
    else if v < target
      lo = mid + 1
    else
      hi = mid - 1
    end
  end
  found
end

fn main() -> Unit
  let line1 = match io.read_line()
  when Some(s)
    s
  when None
    ""
  end
  let line2 = match io.read_line()
  when Some(s)
    s
  when None
    ""
  end
  let xs = parse_ints(line1)
  let targets = parse_ints(line2)
  let t = match targets.get(0)
  when Some(v)
    v
  when None
    0
  end
  match binary_search(xs, t)
  when Some(i)
    print("index=#{i}")
  when None
    print("not_found")
  end
end
